sourceMovie.title: The Tree of Life
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Tree of Life works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: What Dreams May Come
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Tree of Life clicked for you, What Dreams May Come (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and fantasy, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: CJ7
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Tree of Life clicked for you, CJ7 (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Labyrinth
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Tree of Life clicked for you, Labyrinth (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Tree of Life?
faq.answer: Try What Dreams May Come, CJ7, Labyrinth—each shares DNA with The Tree of Life while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Tree of Life?
faq.answer: The Tree of Life (2011) is best described as Drama, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Tree of Life worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
